output:
**(B)**.

These are all instances of **throughput-quality inversion** — optimizing a rate metric (units processed per time) degrades first-pass quality, which creates a rework loop (readmissions, repeat calls, regressions) that feeds demand back into the system's intake. The measured metric improves while the system's actual throughput of *resolved* work decreases.

The key structural element is the **hidden feedback loop**: the output of the "fast" process re-enters as new input, so the system is partially running in circles while the dashboard says it's speeding up.
